CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Continued To Sep-09-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2025 Set For Case Management Conference On Dec-10-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Certain Of Plaintiff'S Request For Prodction Of Documents Or Things, Set No. Two From Defendant Cecil Russell; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Jason Brown, 415-652-4202 Plaintiff In Pro Per.
Joseph Angelo, Esq. 916-290-7778 Gale Angelo Johnson & Pruett for Defendants.

ARGUED. Defense counsel Mr. Angelo contests sanctions. Mr. Angelo states verifications are included. Mr. Brown objects. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JASON BROWN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Certain Of Plaintiff'S Request For Prodction Of Documents Or Things, Set No. Two From Defendant Cecil Russell; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling except for sanctions. The Court deletes the $60 dollar sanction.

Plaintiff Jason Brown's motion to compel further responses from defendant Cecil Russell to requests 1-5, 9-12, 14-18 and 21 of his second set of requests for production of documents is granted and denied as to requests 1-5, 9-12, 14-15, and 21. Mr. Russell must provide verified further code-compliant responses to requests 16-18 and produce all responsive documents to those requests. Because Mr. Russell provided a verification for his original responses with his opposition papers to this motion, the portion of the motion seeking a verification is moot. Mr. Russell's responses to requests 1-5 , 9-12 and 21 that after a diligent search he has already produced all documents in his possession is sufficient compliance with his discovery obligations. Mr. Brown may question Mr. Russell about the scope of his asserted diligent search at Mr. Russell's deposition. Mr. Brown has not shown that there is any discovery relevance to his requests 14-15 seeking documents about particular Paycheck Protection Program loans, which are not mentioned in his complaint. Mr. Brown's requests 16-18 seeking documents relating to him that are maintained in compliance with San Francisco Labor and Employment Code 11.6 and complaints of sexually inappropriate behavior by other employees meet the standard for discovery relevance and are not overbroad nor has Mr. Russell shown that any of those requests would be unduly burdensome to comply with.

Mr. Angelo to prepare a proposed order approved as to form.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses, Without Objections, To Request For Production Of Documents Or Things, Set No. 1 And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Santions Against Defendant And Defense Counsel In The Sum Of $60; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF JASON BROWN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses, Without Objections, To Request For Production Of Documents Or Things, Set No. 1 And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Santions Against Defendant And Defense Counsel In The Sum Of $60.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion denied. First, the moving party has not filed a separate statement as required by Cal. Rule of Court 3.1345(a). Second, many of the requests are overbroad and call for production of confidential or private information. Plaintiff may serve new requests that cure the defects in the existing requests.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)
